hooks.prompt:
A git commit is about to run in the keeply-suite monorepo. Your job: auto-review staged keeply-blog content for BWF voice / E-E-A-T issues that deterministic lint cannot catch.

STEP 1 — cheap gate. Run: git diff --cached --name-only . If NO staged path starts with "apps/blog/content/", respond exactly "PASS: no blog content staged." and stop. Do not read anything else. Do not block.

STEP 2 — only if blog content is staged. Identify the staged slug(s) from paths shaped apps/blog/content/{locale}/post/{slug}/... . For each unique slug, read the master draft: prefer apps/blog/content/zh-tw/post/{slug}/index.md, otherwise apps/blog/content/english/post/{slug}/index.md . Then read the rubric file .claude/agents/bwf-voice-reviewer.md and apply it to each draft.

STEP 3 — report in Traditional Chinese, concise: list findings as P0 / P1 / observation, each with location (H2 or first sentence) + original text + why + fix. Lead with the concrete example, not the abstract rule.

VERDICT RULE (be conservative): Block the commit ONLY on an unambiguous P0 positioning red line — Keeply framed as a Git-for-non-developers tool (P0.2), Git terminology leaking into prose or UI strings (P0.1), or the false claim that Cmd+S triggers Keeply (mechanism error). For every judgment call, every P1, every observation, and any time you are unsure: ALLOW the commit and just surface the findings. Never block on a maybe. Finish with exactly one final line: "VERDICT: ALLOW" or "VERDICT: BLOCK - <one-line reason>".
